name: Ashwagandha
safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: Back to the Roots: Safety and Tolerability of Standardised Ashwagandha Root Extract
sources.citation: 23 studies; 2317 healthy adults; 125-600 mg/day; up to 180 days; no serious adverse events attributable to standardized root-only extract; long-term data limited

sources.title: Does Ashwagandha supplementation have a beneficial effect on the management of anxiety and stress?
Effect of Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) extract on sleep: A systematic review and meta-analysis
Ashwagandha-induced liver injury: A case series from Iceland and the US DILIN
sources.citation: 5 cases; cholestatic/mixed liver injury; jaundice/pruritus; latency 2-12 weeks; normalized in most within 1-5 months

sources.title: Evaluating the Safety of Ashwagandha as a Food Additive
sources.citation: Caution for pregnancy, thyroid disorders, liver/kidney dysfunction; long-term safety concerns

sources.title: Effects of Withania somnifera Extract in Chronically Stressed Adults
sources.citation: Root-and-leaf aqueous extract; 125/250/500 mg/day; 8 weeks; 98 analyzed adults with high stress

sources.title: Use short-term, cautious structure/function wording; include liver, thyroid, pregnancy, sedative, antidiabetes, antihypertensive, and immunosuppressant cautions.
Efficacy and Safety of Ashwagandha Root Extract on Cognitive Functions in Healthy, Stressed Adults
sources.citation: Sustained-release root extract capsule 300 mg/day for 90 days; 125 completed

claimMap.claim: Ashwagandha extract may support sleep quality, with stronger signals in insomnia populations at ≥600 mg/day for ≥8 weeks.
claimMap.notes: Ashwagandha extract may support sleep quality, with stronger signals in insomnia populations at ≥600 mg/day for ≥8 weeks.

qualifiers.direction: small significant sleep support signal; subgroup dose/duration clue
qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Effects more prominent with ≥600 mg/day and duration ≥8 weeks
qualifiers.population: Adults; subgroup more prominent in adults with insomnia

claimMap.claim: Standardized single-ingredient root-only ashwagandha extract appears generally tolerated in healthy adults at studied doses and durations, but do not imply long-term safety.
claimMap.notes: Standardized single-ingredient root-only ashwagandha extract appears generally tolerated in healthy adults at studied doses and durations, but do not imply long-term safety.

claimMap.claim: A specific aqueous root-and-leaf ashwagandha extract was studied at 125–500 mg/day for 8 weeks for mild-to-moderate chronic stress support.
claimMap.notes: A specific aqueous root-and-leaf ashwagandha extract was studied at 125–500 mg/day for 8 weeks for mild-to-moderate chronic stress support.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Aqueous Withania somnifera root-and-leaf extract; 125, 250, or 500 mg/day for 8 weeks

claimMap.claim: Ashwagandha extract may modestly improve sleep quality, especially in insomnia studies and longer/higher-dose protocols, but long-term safety evidence is limited.
claimMap.notes: Ashwagandha extract may modestly improve sleep quality, especially in insomnia studies and longer/higher-dose protocols, but long-term safety evidence is limited.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Ashwagandha extract; stronger subgroup signal at >=600 mg/day and >=8 weeks

claimMap.claim: Across RCTs, ashwagandha showed stress/anxiety support signals, with stress effects most interpretable around 300–600 mg/day; certainty was low.
claimMap.notes: Across RCTs, ashwagandha showed stress/anxiety support signals, with stress effects most interpretable around 300–600 mg/day; certainty was low.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Stress dose-response signal favored 300–600 mg/day; anxiety analysis included wide dose range not directly interchangeable
qualifiers.population: Adults in RCTs; age range 25–48 reported

claimMap.claim: A sustained-release ashwagandha root extract at 300 mg/day for about 90 days was studied for stress, sleep quality, memory, and focus in healthy stressed adults.
claimMap.notes: A sustained-release ashwagandha root extract at 300 mg/day for about 90 days was studied for stress, sleep quality, memory, and focus in healthy stressed adults.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Sustained-release ashwagandha root extract capsule 300 mg once daily for 90 days
qualifiers.population: Healthy stressed adults age 20–55 with PSS 14–24

claimMap.claim: Use short-term, cautious structure/function wording; include liver, thyroid, pregnancy, sedative, antidiabetes, antihypertensive, and immunosuppressant cautions.
claimMap.notes: Use short-term, cautious structure/function wording; include liver, thyroid, pregnancy, sedative, antidiabetes, antihypertensive, and immunosuppressant cautions.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: NIH notes up to about 3 months tolerance; long-term unknown

claimMap.claim: Ashwagandha should carry caution language for pregnancy, thyroid disorders, liver/kidney dysfunction, and long-term safety uncertainty.
claimMap.notes: Ashwagandha should carry caution language for pregnancy, thyroid disorders, liver/kidney dysfunction, and long-term safety uncertainty.

claimMap.claim: Ashwagandha has rare case-report evidence of clinically apparent liver injury, usually cholestatic or mixed with jaundice/pruritus.
claimMap.notes: Ashwagandha has rare case-report evidence of clinically apparent liver injury, usually cholestatic or mixed with jaundice/pruritus.

claimMap.claim: Ashwagandha may reduce stress and anxiety compared with placebo, but certainty is low and heterogeneity is high.
claimMap.notes: Ashwagandha may reduce stress and anxiety compared with placebo, but certainty is low and heterogeneity is high.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Stress signal around 300-600 mg/day in dose-response analysis
